arguments.title: [session-api][handoff] Populate resolved validation outcome and new_entities at handoff creation
arguments.description:
# Goal

Populate the resolved validation `outcome` and `new_entities` when a handoff record is created (subticket 2 of `6431985e`). Depends on the model fields from subticket 1.

# Problem (root cause, verified)

`create_handoff_record` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1343`) calls `resolve_validation_gates(&context, validation, false)`. Inside `resolve_validation_gates` (`store.rs:1614`), if `required_specs` (workflow validation nodes marked `Required`) is empty, it returns the caller gates **verbatim with `outcome=None`**. Caller-supplied gates that are not backed by a required workflow node therefore never get an authoritative outcome — exactly why handoff `816f0807` persisted `outcome=None`.

# Solution Design

1. In `resolve_validation_gates`, after handling required workflow specs, also resolve an authoritative `outcome` for every caller-supplied gate by querying test-api (`test_store.list_executions` with `validation_spec_id`, newest first) and setting `outcome = validation_outcome_label(latest)`. Keep the existing `strict_required` fail-closed behavior for required specs unchanged; this only *fills in* display outcomes for non-required gates (never promotes them to completion authority).
2. In `create_handoff_record`, compute `new_entities` for this run: entities created/owned by the session's agent id (board `owned_files` → ticket ids; plus any explicitly supplied created URNs) and set the field added in subticket 1.
3. Populate `summary`/`remaining`/`decisions`/`blockers` from the request payload threaded in subticket 3 (leave empty here if not supplied).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A handoff created for a run that recorded a validation execution persists a non-`None` resolved `outcome` for that gate, even when it is not a required workflow node.
2. `new_entities` reflects tickets created/owned during the run.
3. `strict_required` semantics for required validation nodes are unchanged (regression test).
4. Focused test asserts outcome + new_entities population at `create_handoff_record`.

# Traceability

- Parent: `6431985e-e729-426b-9f91-66ad4b1c6fe6`.
- Depends on model subticket (fields).
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913` (AC5).

tool_arguments_json.title: [session-api][handoff] Thread handoff narrative fields through CLI, MCP, and /handoff prompt
tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Let agents supply structured handoff narrative through the surfaces instead of pasting prose into chat (subticket 3 of `6431985e`). Depends on model (subticket 1) and population (subticket 2).

# Code touchpoints

- Handoff entrypoints: `create_handoff_result` / `render_handoff_terminal` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1383+`).
- Terminal render: `render_handoff_record_terminal` (`store.rs:2108`) must print the new narrative/blockers/new_entities sections.
- session-cli `handoff` command and session-mcp `session_handoff` tool input schemas.
- The generated `/handoff` prompt template.

# Solution Design

1. Add optional `--summary`, `--remaining` (repeatable), `--decisions` (repeatable), `--blockers` (repeatable) inputs to the session-cli `handoff` command and matching fields to the session-mcp `session_handoff` input.
2. Thread those into the request consumed by `create_handoff_record` (populated in subticket 2).
3. Extend `render_handoff_record_terminal` to render the narrative, blockers, and `new_entities` sections.
4. Update the `/handoff` prompt to instruct agents to fill these structured fields rather than free prose.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. `handoff` via CLI and MCP accepts and persists summary/remaining/decisions/blockers.
2. Terminal render shows the new sections when populated and omits them when empty.
3. The `/handoff` prompt references the structured fields.
4. Focused test covers CLI/MCP input → persisted record round-trip.

# Traceability

- Parent: `6431985e-e729-426b-9f91-66ad4b1c6fe6`.
- Depends on population subticket.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913` (AC5).

tool_arguments_json.title: [session-api][handoff] Flatten SessionWorkflowSnapshot and drop denormalized live_ticket_state
tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Remove the two structural smells in the handoff workflow snapshot (subticket 1 of `96f9ffaa`) — low-risk schema cleanup, independent of the delta work.

# Problem (verified)

- `SessionWorkflowSnapshot` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:226`) has `pub workflow: SessionWorkflowGraph`, so it serializes as double-nested `workflow.workflow.{nodes,edges}` inside the handoff record's own `workflow` field.
- `SessionWorkflowNodeResolution.live_ticket_state` (`model.rs:212`) is denormalized: it re-states state already derivable at read time from node status + the live ticket resolver (`workflow_snapshot`, `store.rs:1147`, rebuilds it every call anyway).

# Solution Design

1. Flatten the snapshot: either `#[serde(flatten)]` the graph or rename `workflow` → hoist `nodes`/`edges` to the snapshot level, eliminating `workflow.workflow`. Update `workflow_snapshot` (`store.rs:1147`) and all readers (`workflow_render_terminal`, mermaid render, handoff render).
2. Drop `live_ticket_state` from the persisted `SessionWorkflowNodeResolution`; recompute it on demand where readers need it (the resolver is already invoked in `workflow_snapshot`). If a persisted resolution list is still required, keep only `node_id` + diagnostics.
3. Provide a back-compat deserialization path so existing records with `workflow.workflow` and `live_ticket_state` still load.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Newly persisted snapshots no longer contain a nested `workflow.workflow` object.
2. `live_ticket_state` is no longer persisted; live state is derived at read time.
3. Existing records with the old shape still deserialize.
4. Workflow render / mermaid / handoff render output is unchanged for a given graph.
5. Focused test covers old-shape load + new-shape round-trip.

# Traceability

- Parent: `96f9ffaa-6514-480a-afbd-b345cc206863`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

tool_arguments_json.title: [session-api][handoff] Add narrative/blockers/new_entities fields to SessionHandoffRecord model
tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Extend the `SessionHandoffRecord` schema with the durable knowledge-transfer payload (subticket 1 of `6431985e`). Model + serde only — no population logic.

# Code touchpoints (verified)

- `SessionHandoffRecord` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:251`) currently: `handoff_id, workspace_session_id, outgoing_run_id, created_at, resume_command, pinned_entities, workflow, validation`.
- `SessionHandoffResult` (`model.rs:265`) wraps `record + record_path + render`.

# Solution Design

Add to `SessionHandoffRecord`, all `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "...")]` for backward compatibility:

- `summary: String` (`skip_serializing_if = "String::is_empty"`) — freeform run outcome.
- `remaining: Vec<String>` — remaining/next-action items.
- `decisions: Vec<String>` — decisions taken this run.
- `blockers: Vec<String>` — open blockers for the successor.
- `new_entities: Vec<SessionPinnedEntityHeader>` (or a lighter `Vec<String>` of URNs) — entities created/owned this run.

Do not change any writer yet; population is subticket 2. Only the struct, serde attributes, and a round-trip test.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New fields exist on `SessionHandoffRecord` with serde defaults and skip-if-empty.
2. An old record JSON without the new fields deserializes with empty defaults.
3. A round-trip test asserts populated values serialize and deserialize identically, and that empty fields are omitted from JSON.

# Traceability

- Parent: `6431985e-e729-426b-9f91-66ad4b1c6fe6`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913` (AC5).

tool_arguments_json.title: [session-api][handoff] Delta/baseline handoff serialization with full reconstruct path
tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Make successive handoffs persist a compact delta instead of re-serializing ~97% identical bytes (subticket 2 of `96f9ffaa`). Depends on the schema cleanup (subticket 1) so the delta is defined over the flattened shape.

# Problem (verified)

Incoming handoff `02d19bac` (8562 B) and outgoing `816f0807` (8561 B) differ by ~10 substantive lines yet nearly all bytes are re-serialized verbatim. `create_handoff_record` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1343`) always writes a full record; there is no baseline reference.

# Solution Design

1. Add an optional delta representation to `SessionHandoffRecord`: `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] baseline_handoff_id: Option<String>` plus `changed_nodes: Vec<...>`, `added_pins: Vec<...>`, `removed_pins: Vec<...>`.
2. On `create_handoff_record`, load the predecessor handoff (via lineage) as baseline; if present, diff workflow nodes and pins and persist only the delta with `baseline_handoff_id` set. If no baseline, persist a full record.
3. Provide a `materialize_handoff(handoff_id)` reader that walks `baseline_handoff_id` back to the nearest full record and replays deltas to reconstruct the complete `SessionHandoffRecord`. All existing readers (render, MCP get) go through materialization.
4. Guard against broken baseline chains (missing baseline → fall back to treating as full / error clearly).

# Non-goals

- Narrative/blockers fields (parent `6431985e`).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A subsequent handoff over an unchanged workflow persists a delta significantly smaller than a full re-serialization.
2. `materialize_handoff` reconstructs the full record from baseline + deltas; output equals what a full record would have contained.
3. A broken/missing baseline chain is detected and reported, not silently wrong.
4. Existing full records still load and materialize (identity).
5. Focused tests cover full→delta→materialize and broken-chain cases.

# Traceability

- Parent: `96f9ffaa-6514-480a-afbd-b345cc206863`.
- Depends on schema-cleanup subticket.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

tool_arguments_json.title: [session-api][handoff] Flatten SessionWorkflowSnapshot and drop denormalized live_ticket_state
tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Remove the two structural smells in the handoff workflow snapshot (subticket 1 of `96f9ffaa`) — low-risk schema cleanup, independent of the delta work.

# Problem (verified)

- `SessionWorkflowSnapshot` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:226`) has `pub workflow: SessionWorkflowGraph`, so it serializes as double-nested `workflow.workflow.{nodes,edges}` inside the handoff record's own `workflow` field.
- `SessionWorkflowNodeResolution.live_ticket_state` (`model.rs:212`) is denormalized: it re-states state already derivable at read time from node status + the live ticket resolver (`workflow_snapshot`, `store.rs:1147`, rebuilds it every call anyway).

# Solution Design

1. Flatten the snapshot: either `#[serde(flatten)]` the graph or rename `workflow` → hoist `nodes`/`edges` to the snapshot level, eliminating `workflow.workflow`. Update `workflow_snapshot` (`store.rs:1147`) and all readers (`workflow_render_terminal`, mermaid render, handoff render).
2. Drop `live_ticket_state` from the persisted `SessionWorkflowNodeResolution`; recompute it on demand where readers need it (the resolver is already invoked in `workflow_snapshot`). If a persisted resolution list is still required, keep only `node_id` + diagnostics.
3. Provide a back-compat deserialization path so existing records with `workflow.workflow` and `live_ticket_state` still load.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Newly persisted snapshots no longer contain a nested `workflow.workflow` object.
2. `live_ticket_state` is no longer persisted; live state is derived at read time.
3. Existing records with the old shape still deserialize.
4. Workflow render / mermaid / handoff render output is unchanged for a given graph.
5. Focused test covers old-shape load + new-shape round-trip.

# Traceability

- Parent: `96f9ffaa-6514-480a-afbd-b345cc206863`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

tool_arguments_json.title: [session-api][handoff] Add narrative/blockers/new_entities fields to SessionHandoffRecord model
tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Extend the `SessionHandoffRecord` schema with the durable knowledge-transfer payload (subticket 1 of `6431985e`). Model + serde only — no population logic.

# Code touchpoints (verified)

- `SessionHandoffRecord` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:251`) currently: `handoff_id, workspace_session_id, outgoing_run_id, created_at, resume_command, pinned_entities, workflow, validation`.
- `SessionHandoffResult` (`model.rs:265`) wraps `record + record_path + render`.

# Solution Design

Add to `SessionHandoffRecord`, all `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "...")]` for backward compatibility:

- `summary: String` (`skip_serializing_if = "String::is_empty"`) — freeform run outcome.
- `remaining: Vec<String>` — remaining/next-action items.
- `decisions: Vec<String>` — decisions taken this run.
- `blockers: Vec<String>` — open blockers for the successor.
- `new_entities: Vec<SessionPinnedEntityHeader>` (or a lighter `Vec<String>` of URNs) — entities created/owned this run.

Do not change any writer yet; population is subticket 2. Only the struct, serde attributes, and a round-trip test.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New fields exist on `SessionHandoffRecord` with serde defaults and skip-if-empty.
2. An old record JSON without the new fields deserializes with empty defaults.
3. A round-trip test asserts populated values serialize and deserialize identically, and that empty fields are omitted from JSON.

# Traceability

- Parent: `6431985e-e729-426b-9f91-66ad4b1c6fe6`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913` (AC5).

The dependencies flow from the parent tickets down to their subtickets, and then between subtickets where one's output feeds into another—like how population depends on the model fields being defined first, and threading depends on population being complete. arguments.description:
# Goal

Make `SessionHandoffRecord` carry the durable knowledge-transfer payload that spec `8c880efc` AC5 and ticket `0647a212` scope already require, instead of stranding it in the chat transcript. **This is now a tracker parent decomposed into 3 subtickets.**

# Problem (evidence)

`SessionHandoffRecord` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:251`) has `handoff_id, workspace_session_id, outgoing_run_id, created_at, resume_command, pinned_entities, workflow, validation` and no `blockers` or narrative fields.

- Observed record `816f0807-...json` persisted `validation: [{validation_spec_id, required:true}]` with `outcome=None` and no blockers; the entire findings/decisions/next-actions handoff lived only in session `beca8ec5` transcript.
- Root cause of `outcome=None`: `create_handoff_record` (`store.rs:1343`) calls `resolve_validation_gates(&context, validation, false)`, and `resolve_validation_gates` (`store.rs:1614`) only populates `outcome` for gates backed by a *required workflow validation node*; caller gates with no matching node pass through with `outcome=None`.

# Decomposition

1. `a6f17580` — Add narrative/blockers/new_entities fields to `SessionHandoffRecord` model (serde + round-trip).
2. `e8bdb7cf` — Populate resolved validation `outcome` + `new_entities` at handoff creation (depends on 1).
3. `f77e35d8` — Thread narrative fields through CLI/MCP/`/handoff` prompt (depends on 2).

Subticket ordering: 1 → 2 → 3. Parent `depends_on` all three.

# Acceptance Criteria (parent, satisfied by subtickets)

1. A persisted handoff record includes non-empty `summary`, `blockers`, and resolved validation `outcome` when the run recorded validation.
2. `new_entities` reflects tickets/specs created or owned during the run.
3. Old records without the new fields still deserialize.
4. Focused tests assert the new fields persist and round-trip.

# Non-goals

- Delta/baseline serialization (tracked in `96f9ffaa`).
- Auto-generating narrative from the transcript.

# Traceability

- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913` (AC5, Positions handoff row).
- Predecessor: `0647a212-9d2e-4943-9627-f854ce3f14c4` (unfinished contract).
- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.

# Goal

Reduce handoff record size and remove structural smells so inter-agent records are compact and clean. **This is now a tracker parent decomposed into 2 subtickets** because it bundles one low-risk cleanup and one larger format change.

# Problem (evidence)

Incoming handoff `02d19bac` (8562 B) and outgoing `816f0807` (8561 B) differ by ~10 substantive lines yet ~97% of bytes are re-serialized verbatim. `SessionWorkflowSnapshot` (`model.rs:226`) serializes as double-nested `workflow.workflow.{nodes,edges}` and carries `resolutions[].live_ticket_state` (`model.rs:212`) already derivable from node status + the live resolver in `workflow_snapshot` (`store.rs:1147`).

# Decomposition

1. `68c8a5ef` — Flatten `SessionWorkflowSnapshot` (remove `workflow.workflow`) and drop denormalized `live_ticket_state`. Low risk, no format negotiation.
2. `89d1f983` — Delta/baseline handoff serialization with a full `materialize_handoff` reconstruct path (depends on 1, so the delta is defined over the flattened shape).

Subticket ordering: 1 → 2. Parent `depends_on` both.

# Acceptance Criteria (parent, satisfied by subtickets)

1. A subsequent handoff persists a delta significantly smaller than a full re-serialization for an unchanged workflow.
2. Full workflow can be reconstructed from baseline + deltas.
3. Snapshot no longer double-nests `workflow` and no longer stores redundant live ticket state.
4. Existing records still load.

# Non-goals

- Narrative/blockers fields (`6431985e`).

# Traceability

- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.

data_json.content: All edges wired. Finally, update the two parent tickets to reflect their decomposition, then verify graph integrity.

- Root cause of `outcome=None`: `create_handoff_record` (`store.rs:1343`) calls `resolve_validation_gates(&context, validation, false)`, and `resolve_validation_gates` (`store.rs:1614`) only populates `outcome` for gates backed by a *required workflow validation node*; caller gates with no matching node pass through with `outcome=None`.

# Decomposition

1. `a6f17580` — Add narrative/blockers/new_entities fields to `SessionHandoffRecord` model (serde + round-trip).
2. `e8bdb7cf` — Populate resolved validation `outcome` + `new_entities` at handoff creation (depends on 1).
3. `f77e35d8` — Thread narrative fields through CLI/MCP/`/handoff` prompt (depends on 2).

Subticket ordering: 1 → 2 → 3. Parent `depends_on` all three.

# Acceptance Criteria (parent, satisfied by subtickets)

1. A persisted handoff record includes non-empty `summary`, `blockers`, and resolved validation `outcome` when the run recorded validation.
2. `new_entities` reflects tickets/specs created or owned during the run.
3. Old records without the new fields still deserialize.
4. Focused tests assert the new fields persist and round-trip.

# Non-goals

- Delta/baseline serialization (tracked in `96f9ffaa`).
- Auto-generating narrative from the transcript.

# Goal

Reduce handoff record size and remove structural smells so inter-agent records are compact and clean. **This is now a tracker parent decomposed into 2 subtickets** because it bundles one low-risk cleanup and one larger format change.

# Problem (evidence)

Incoming handoff `02d19bac` (8562 B) and outgoing `816f0807` (8561 B) differ by ~10 substantive lines yet ~97% of bytes are re-serialized verbatim. `SessionWorkflowSnapshot` (`model.rs:226`) serializes as double-nested `workflow.workflow.{nodes,edges}` and carries `resolutions[].live_ticket_state` (`model.rs:212`) already derivable from node status + the live resolver in `workflow_snapshot` (`store.rs:1147`).

# Decomposition

1. `68c8a5ef` — Flatten `SessionWorkflowSnapshot` (remove `workflow.workflow`) and drop denormalized `live_ticket_state`. Low risk, no format negotiation.
2. `89d1f983` — Delta/baseline handoff serialization with a full `materialize_handoff` reconstruct path (depends on 1, so the delta is defined over the flattened shape).

Subticket ordering: 1 → 2. Parent `depends_on` both.

# Acceptance Criteria (parent, satisfied by subtickets)

1. A subsequent handoff persists a delta significantly smaller than a full re-serialization for an unchanged workflow.
2. Full workflow can be reconstructed from baseline + deltas.
3. Snapshot no longer double-nests `workflow` and no longer stores redundant live ticket state.
4. Existing records still load.

# Non-goals

- Narrative/blockers fields (`6431985e`).

# Traceability

- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.

2. `89d1f983` — Delta/baseline handoff serialization with a full `materialize_handoff` reconstruct path (depends on 1, so the delta is defined over the flattened shape).

# Acceptance Criteria (parent, satisfied by subtickets)

2. Full workflow can be reconstructed from baseline + deltas.
3. Snapshot no longer double-nests `workflow` and no longer stores redundant live ticket state.
4. Existing records still load.

- Narrative/blockers fields (`6431985e`).

data_json.content:
I found that we are creating "session workspaces" and placing handoffs in those workspaces. This is a complexity I want to avoid. Handoffs should be owned by the session and sessions should all live in the same workspace. the "workspace" is defined by the the .session/ folder level, which is the root workspace of the session.

Can you verify that this design can work without compromising any features, or are there any reasons why we should keep separate workspaces within the .session workspace or keep handoffs separately?

We also want these artifacts to be persisted in git for later feedback and research loops.

events.reasoning_text:
I'm mapping out the directory structure for the session storage system. There's a sessions folder holding captured transcripts and events organized by session ID, while a separate runtime folder tracks the active workspace session and stores individual workspace contexts with pins and work data.

The key insight is that `session_id` and `workspace_session_id` are two separate ID spaces serving different purposes. The session ID comes from the Copilot capture hook and represents a single captured session, whereas the workspace session ID is a separately-generated UUID that persists across multiple runs through lineage tracking. The user wants to flatten this hierarchy so handoffs and other artifacts are owned directly by the session rather than nested under a per-workspace layer, but the challenge is that the runtime workspace needs to support resuming runs within the same workspace context. offs are owned by "the session", but I need to clarify what granularity they mean. Since they're saying "sessions should all live in the same workspace," they're objecting to the `runtime/workspaces/<id>/` structure and want something flatter like `.session/sessions/<session_id>/handoffs/` or `.session/handoffs/`. The runtime workspace layer exists for run lineage and resume continuity—multiple runs share pins, workflow, and handoff history—plus the `active_workspace_session.json` pointer and per-workspace finish immutability. I'm checking whether `workspace_session_id` is 1:1 with a captured `session_id`, and whether resume reuses the same workspace but creates a different session. ity and store runtime context + handoffs alongside the session? The tricky part is handling the 1:N relationship where a resume spawns a new captured session but maintains the same runtime thread. Let me look at the actual terminal history to ground this: the user ran `session resume --workspace-session-id 03baab6c-... --predecessor-run-id 62d6ce97-...`, and the handoffs lived in `.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs`, so that workspace_session_id persists across resumes. The question is whether there's a real architectural reason to keep the run workspace separate from captured sessions. Looking at what actually lives at each layer: the workspace holds runtime context like pins and workflow, run lineage, handoff snapshots per run, finish records, and the active_workspace_session.json pointer for hookless resolution. Meanwhile captured sessions contain the manifest, transcript, and events. These genuinely have different lifecycles — captured sessions are append-only transcripts tied to individual Copilot conversations, while the runtime workspace is mutable and resumable across multiple captured sessions.

Now I'm wondering whether a run maps 1:1 to a captured session. A run gets created on init or resume, and a captured session gets created when the hook captures data — but are they actually linked? I need to check if the runtime context references session IDs or if there's a join between these two ID spaces through something like SessionLinks in the model. If each run corresponds to exactly one captured session, then handoffs could belong to the captured session layer, but the runtime context itself (pins and workflow) accumulates across the entire thread. Let me verify whether the run lineage actually stores the captured session ID to understand how these two systems connect. I need to trace through the SessionLinks to see if workspace_session_id could just be aliased to session_id, and check whether `.session/` is properly git-ignored for persistence.

More importantly, there's no persisted mapping between a captured session and a runtime run — the only connection is implicit through timing and the active workspace session pointer, which matters for the user's proposal about session-owned handoffs since we'd need to define which session owns what.

I'm also noticing there are multiple runtime workspaces already under `.session/`, and I need to verify whether `.session/` is actually being tracked in git or ignored. The `git check-ignore` returned nothing, so it's not ignored, but I should check the `.gitignore` files to be sure. More importantly, there's no persisted mapping between a captured session and a runtime run — the only connection is implicit through timing and the active workspace session pointer, which matters for the user's proposal about session-owned handoffs since we'd need to define which session owns what.

events.reasoning_text:
Looking at the `.gitignore` configuration, I can see the current setup explicitly tracks the captured transcripts under `.session/sessions/` (222 files) while ignoring the runtime directory, which means the session data is being persisted in git.

The key issue is that handoffs live under `.session/runtime/workspaces/<wsid>/handoffs/`, which falls under the ignored `runtime/` subtree — so handoffs aren't currently persisted to git at all. To achieve the goal of persisting these artifacts for feedback and research loops, I'd need to either move handoffs out of the ignored runtime directory or update the ignore rules to include them.

I'm also noticing that multiple runtime workspaces already coexist in the repository, suggesting there's a concurrent-parallel requirement where independent continuity threads operate simultaneously.

The user seems to be conflating two layers: the store/workspace (`.session/` itself, which is singular) and the runtime workspace (the `runtime/workspaces/<id>/` structure that represents a continuity thread spanning multiple resumes). When they mention "session workspaces," they're referring to that runtime layer and want to flatten the structure so handoffs are owned directly by the session rather than nested under a separate workspace ID.

The real question is whether collapsing this design would compromise the ability to maintain multiple independent continuity threads in parallel, or if there are other architectural reasons to keep the runtime workspace layer separate.

Looking at what the runtime workspace actually provides: it's the natural home for cross-run cumulative state and lineage tracking. If we collapse to just the captured session (the transcript), we lose that continuity anchor—unless we redefine what "the session" means to be the runtime thread itself rather than the captured transcript. That would make the captured transcripts children of the thread instead.

The store already supports multiple parallel threads (three are currently present), so "one workspace for all sessions" is already satisfied at the store level. The real work is defining the relationship between sessions and threads, since today they have no join field. We need to establish how handoffs owned by the session would actually reference the thread. Handoffs are really just snapshots the thread creates, so they logically belong with the session thread rather than being treated as separate runtime state. My conclusion is that flattening can work without sacrificing functionality—the key is treating the runtime workspace as the session continuity thread and aligning the identity model. The `workspace_session_id` directory isn't providing real isolation beyond "one directory per continuity thread," which you still need to avoid collisions. What I can eliminate is the confusing `runtime/workspaces/` naming, the git-ignore pattern, and the misaligned ID spaces. The real reason to keep a per-thread directory is for managing pins, workflow, and run data.

The git persistence piece requires updating `.gitignore` rules since the current setup excludes all runtime and handoff artifacts, and ideally moving handoffs and context out of `runtime/` (which signals ephemeral) into the tracked session tree. There's also an inconsistency in `memory-api/.gitignore` where `.session/` is ignored entirely in that submodule, which doesn't align with the root configuration.

The key distinction isn't cleanly "runtime vs sessions" — it's that durable artifacts like handoffs, context, finish state, transcripts, and events should be tracked, while local pointers like `active_workspace_session.json` and locks remain genuinely ephemeral and git-ignored. So the answer is yes, this can work, but I need to preserve the per-thread container structure, maintain lineage integrity, and keep finish state immutable.

Let me verify the critical path: `resolve_workspace_session_id` does fall back to `active_workspace_session.json` as the "current thread" pointer, which is the mechanism that makes handoffs resolvable without an explicit id. That's sound. Now I'll structure a thorough analytical response covering the actual layout, why handoffs are currently separate, what must be preserved, the recommended target structure, git persistence changes, and the migration risks.

data_json.content:
I've traced the full layout and the git-tracking rules. Here's the verdict.

## What the layout actually is today

There are **two completely decoupled ID spaces** under `.session/`:

| Layer | Path | Identity | Lifecycle | Contents |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Captured session | `.session/sessions/<session_id>/` | `session_id` (Copilot conversation id) | one per hook capture (append-only) | `session.json`, `transcript.json`, `events.json` |
| Runtime "workspace" | `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` | `workspace_session_id` (fresh UUID) | one per **continuity thread**, spans many resumes | `context.json` (pins + workflow + run lineage), `handoffs/`, `finish.json` |

The critical fact: `SessionRuntimeContext` (model.rs:51) and `SessionRunLineage` carry **no `session_id`**, and `SessionLinks` (model.rs:298) on a captured session links to tickets/specs/docs/logs but **not** to a runtime workspace or run. The two trees have **zero join field**. A resume (`resume_workspace_context`, store.rs:1420) keeps the same `workspace_session_id` and appends a new `run_id` — while each captured transcript gets a new `session_id`. So the relationship is **1 thread : N transcripts**, and nothing records which transcripts belong to which thread.

## The real reason handoffs are "separate" — and it blocks your git goal

Root `.gitignore`:
```
.session/*
!.session/sessions/
!.session/sessions/default/**
```
This tracks `.session/sessions/**` (222 files tracked) but the un-ignore **never re-includes `runtime/`**. So **handoffs, runtime context, and finish records are git-ignored today** — exactly the artifacts you want for feedback/research loops. (`memory-api/.gitignore:5` is even blunter: `.session/` ignores the whole subtree.) Handoffs aren't separate for a deep architectural reason — they're separate because they were filed under `runtime/` as "ephemeral," which auto-excludes them from git.

## Can the flattening work? Yes — with three things preserved

Your instinct is right, but "all sessions in one workspace" needs precision. The `.session/` store **already** is one workspace (slug `default`); all sessions already live in it. What you're objecting to is the `runtime/workspaces/<id>/` nesting and the git-ignoring. That collapse is safe **if** you keep these, which are the only things the per-workspace directory actually provides:

1. **A per-thread container.** Pins, workflow graph, run lineage, and finish-immutability are *cumulative per continuity thread*. You cannot pour every thread's pins/workflow into one shared file without collisions, and multiple threads already coexist in one repo (I see 3: `03baab6c`, `448a0119`, `6582e9d4` — parallel agents/tickets). So you still need one directory per thread; you just shouldn't call it a "workspace" or bury it under `runtime/`.
2. **Finish immutability** (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`, store.rs:717) is keyed on the per-thread `finish.json`. Keep it per-thread.
3. **Local-only pointers stay ignored.** `active_workspace_session.json` (the "current thread" pointer) and the mutation lock are genuinely machine-local and should remain git-ignored even after handoffs become tracked. The tracked/ignored split isn't "sessions vs runtime" — it's **durable artifacts (transcripts, events, handoffs, context, finish) tracked** vs **local pointers/locks ignored**.

There is **no feature that requires handoffs to live apart from their owning thread.** Moving them to be session/thread-owned is a clean win.

## Recommended target layout

Unify the identity so the thread *is* "the session," and fold transcripts under it as per-run children:

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/        ← "session" == continuity thread (git-tracked)
    context.json                       ← pins + workflow + run lineage
    handoffs/<handoff_id>.json         ← owned by the session
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/
        transcript.json
        events.json
.session/local/                        ← git-ignored
    active_session.json                ← "current" pointer
    *.lock
```

The one design decision you must make (because today it doesn't exist): **give the runtime context a `session_id` and stamp each captured transcript with its owning `session_id` + `run_id`.** Without that join, "handoffs owned by the session" is undefined. That's the actual work — the directory move is trivial by comparison.

## Migration / risk notes

- **Back-compat reads**: existing `runtime/workspaces/<id>/` records must still load; add a fallback resolver during transition.
- **`.gitignore` rewrite** (root + `memory-api`): switch from "ignore all, un-ignore sessions/" to "track `sessions/**` including context+handoffs+finish, ignore only `sessions/*/…` local artifacts + `local/`." Watch repo bloat — `events.json` is large (the 13k-line dedup issue is ticket `67d7c279`), so land that dedup before bulk-tracking events.
- **This directly overlaps your open epic** `effba966` and the handoff tickets I just refined — especially `96f9ffaa` (snapshot schema) and `6431985e` (handoff payload). The identity-unification is a new sibling concern.

## Bottom line

Yes, the design can work without losing features. Keep a **per-thread directory** (rename away from "workspace"/`runtime`), make **handoffs thread-owned**, add the **missing `session_id` join**, and split git-tracking by **durability, not by `sessions/` vs `runtime/`**. The only genuine reasons to keep *any* separation are (a) one container per parallel continuity thread and (b) local-only pointers/locks staying ignored — neither of which requires the current `runtime/workspaces/` nesting or the handoff exclusion from git.

Want me to capture this as a ticket under epic `effba966` (identity unification + layout flattening + git-tracking policy) and, if warranted, a spec update? I did not make any code or ticket changes yet — this is analysis only.

events.reasoning_text:
I need to organize the work into tickets for the flattening design implementation—covering identity unification, layout flattening, and git-tracking policy—plus update the specs and handle markdown handoffs stored alongside JSON. The key constraint is that this flattening track needs to be completed before any other work under the session-api epic, so I'm structuring the dependencies so everything else depends on this track finishing first. I'll structure this as a parent tracker with four ordered subtickets: unify session identity first, then flatten the store layout, add handoff folders with markdown rendering, and finally update the git-tracking policy to ignore local artifacts while tracking durable ones. The later tickets depend on the layout work, so I'll sequence them accordingly and link everything back to the parent.

Now I'm thinking about how to connect this flattening track to the existing handoff-schema tickets — those tickets that directly touch handoff storage should depend on the flattening parent being completed first, so I'll add dependency edges from each of those tickets back to the flattening parent rather than creating a web of individual dependencies. 67d7c279 depends on the flattening parent, and T4 depends on 67d7c279, but the parent also depends on T4 — that creates a cycle. I need to break this by having 67d7c279 depend only on the specific flattening tasks (T1/T2) rather than the parent, so T4 can depend on 67d7c279 without creating a circular dependency. ity. I'll keep the dependency graph clean to avoid cycles: the flattening parent FP has subtickets T1 (identity), T2 (layout+local), T3 (handoff folders+md), and T4 (gitignore policy), where T2 depends on T1, T3 depends on T2, and T4 depends on both T2 and T3 plus the events dedup ticket. Then the downstream handoff-schema and snapshot-schema tickets all depend on FP once the storage model is settled.

Now I'm reconsidering the events dedup ticket — it shouldn't depend on FP since that would create a cycle with T4, but the user wants flattening to gate further work. I'll resolve this by having T4 not depend on the dedup ticket directly, and instead note in T4's description that events dedup should land first.

Actually, wait — events dedup is really a prerequisite enabler for flattening itself, not work that comes after. events.reasoning_text: I'm laying out the ticket structure for this refactoring work—a parent ticket that describes the overall flattening and unification effort, then four subtasks that break down the specific changes needed around session identity, store layout reorganization, and related updates.

arguments.description:
# Goal

Collapse the `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` layer so a session **owns** its runtime context and handoffs directly, all sessions share the single `.session/` store, and durable artifacts are git-tracked for feedback/research loops. **Tracker parent — decomposed into 4 subtickets. This track gates all further session-api handoff/schema work.**

# Problem (evidence)

Verified in `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`:

- Two decoupled ID spaces: captured sessions live at `.session/sessions/<session_id>/` (`paths_for_session_id`, store.rs:1717); runtime threads live at `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` (`runtime_paths_for_workspace`, store.rs:1754) holding `context.json`, `handoffs/`, `finish.json`.
- `SessionRuntimeContext` (`model.rs:51`) and `SessionRunLineage` carry **no `session_id`**; `SessionLinks` (`model.rs:298`) links to tickets/specs/docs/logs but **not** to a runtime thread. There is no join field between the two trees.
- Relationship is 1 thread : N captured transcripts (resume reuses `workspace_session_id`, appends a `run_id`; each capture is a fresh `session_id`).
- Root `.gitignore` (`.session/*` + only un-ignoring `sessions/`) means `runtime/` — **handoffs, context, finish — is git-ignored**, exactly the artifacts wanted for research loops. `memory-api/.gitignore:5` ignores `.session/` entirely.

# Design (target layout)

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/        (git-tracked)
    context.json                       pins + workflow + run lineage
    handoffs/<handoff_id>/handoff.json + handoff.md
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/{transcript.json, events.json}
.session/local/                        (git-ignored)
    active_session.json                current-thread pointer
    *.lock
```

# Decomposition

1. Unify identity (add `session_id` join) — foundational.
2. Flatten layout + relocate local-only pointers/locks, with back-compat readers.
3. Handoffs as folders (`handoff.json` + rendered `handoff.md`).
4. Git-tracking policy (root + `memory-api` `.gitignore`).

# What must be preserved (verified reasons NOT to over-collapse)

- One directory **per continuity thread** (pins/workflow/lineage/finish are cumulative per thread; multiple threads already coexist).
- Finish immutability per thread (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`, store.rs:717).
- Local-only pointers (`active_workspace_session.json`) and the mutation lock stay git-ignored.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A session owns its `context.json`, `handoffs/`, and `finish.json` directly under `sessions/<session_id>/`; `runtime/workspaces/` is no longer written.
2. Runtime context and captured transcripts share the `session_id` join.
3. Handoffs persist as folders with JSON + markdown.
4. Durable artifacts are git-tracked; local pointers/locks are ignored.
5. Existing `runtime/workspaces/` records still load (back-compat).

# Traceability

- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.
- Gates further work: `6431985e`, `96f9ffaa`, `0d3fdba6`, `e731d333`.
arguments.title: [session-api][store] Flatten session store layout, unify identity, and git-track durable artifacts

arguments.description:
# Goal

Establish the missing join between a runtime continuity thread and its captured transcripts so handoffs and context can be session-owned (subticket 1 of the flattening tracker). Foundational — no directory move yet.

# Problem (verified)

- `SessionRuntimeContext` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:51`) has `workspace_session_id` but no `session_id`.
- `SessionRunLineage` (`model.rs:43`) has `run_id` + `predecessor_run_id` but no captured-`session_id`.
- Captured sessions (`SessionLinks`, `model.rs:298`) do not reference a thread/run.
- So "handoff owned by the session" is currently undefined — there is nothing tying a transcript to a runtime thread/run.

# Solution Design

1. Introduce a canonical `session_id` for the continuity thread. Simplest: make `workspace_session_id` the session id (alias) or add `session_id` to `SessionRuntimeContext` populated at `init_runtime_context` (store.rs:608).
2. Stamp each captured transcript with its owning `session_id` + `run_id`: extend `SessionRunLineage` (or the capture manifest) so a run records the captured `session_id`, and the capture path (`capture_copilot_hook`) records the active `run_id`/thread.
3. Provide a resolver `runs_for_session(session_id) -> Vec<run>` and `session_for_run(run_id)` so readers can navigate the join.
4. Keep serde defaults so existing records without the join still deserialize.

# Non-goals

- Moving files on disk (subticket 2).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Runtime context exposes a stable `session_id` for the thread.
2. Each run records its captured `session_id`; the join is navigable both directions.
3. Old records without the join deserialize with empty/derived defaults.
4. Focused test asserts the thread↔run↔transcript join round-trips.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
arguments.title: [session-api][store] Unify session identity: link runtime context to session_id and stamp transcripts

arguments.description:
# Goal

Relocate runtime context/handoffs/finish to be owned directly by `sessions/<session_id>/`, move local-only pointers/locks to `.session/local/`, and remove the `runtime/workspaces/` nesting (subticket 2 of the flattening tracker). Depends on identity unification.

# Problem (verified)

- `runtime_paths_for_workspace` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1754`) builds `runtime/workspaces/<id>/{context.json,handoffs,finish.json}`.
- `runtime_root` (store.rs:1743) and `active_workspace_session_path` (store.rs:1751) place a machine-local "current thread" pointer in the same ignored tree.
- Captured transcripts live at `sessions/<session_id>/` (`paths_for_session_id`, store.rs:1717).

# Target layout

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/
    context.json
    handoffs/…            (folder form in subticket 3)
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/{transcript.json, events.json}
.session/local/
    active_session.json
    *.lock
```

# Solution Design

1. Rework the path builders so `context.json`/`handoffs/`/`finish.json` resolve under `sessions/<session_id>/`; move captured `transcript.json`/`events.json` under `runs/<run_id>/`.
2. Move the active-thread pointer and mutation lock to `.session/local/` (never git-tracked).
3. Add a back-compat read fallback: if `sessions/<id>/context.json` is absent, read the legacy `runtime/workspaces/<id>/` location. Provide an opt-in migration that relocates legacy threads.
4. Update all readers/writers (`persist_runtime_context`, finish, handoff, capture) and CLI/MCP path outputs.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New writes land under `sessions/<session_id>/`; nothing writes to `runtime/workspaces/`.
2. Local pointer + lock live under `.session/local/`.
3. Legacy `runtime/workspaces/` records still load via fallback; migration relocates them.
4. Finish immutability semantics unchanged (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`).
5. Focused tests cover new-layout round-trip + legacy fallback.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on identity subticket.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
arguments.title: [session-api][store] Flatten .session layout under sessions/<session_id>/ and relocate local pointers/locks

arguments.description:
# Goal

Store each handoff as a folder containing the canonical `handoff.json` plus a rendered `handoff.md`, so markdown handoffs sit next to the JSON for human review, feedback, and research loops (subticket 3 of the flattening tracker). Depends on the layout flatten.

# Problem (verified)

- `create_handoff_record` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1343`) writes a single flat file `handoffs/<handoff_id>.json`.
- A terminal render already exists (`render_handoff_record_terminal`, store.rs:2108) but is never persisted — the human-readable form is thrown away.

# Solution Design

1. Change the handoff writer to create a folder `handoffs/<handoff_id>/` and write:
   - `handoff.json` — canonical record (unchanged schema, or delta form per `96f9ffaa`).
   - `handoff.md` — deterministic markdown rendering of the record.
2. Add a `render_handoff_record_markdown(&SessionHandoffRecord) -> String` producing headed sections (summary, remaining, decisions, blockers, pins, workflow, validation, resume command) — reuse the terminal renderer's structure.
3. Update `create_handoff_result.record_path` and CLI/MCP outputs to point at the folder (and/or the `.md`).
4. Back-compat read: still resolve legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json`.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A new handoff persists a folder with both `handoff.json` and `handoff.md`.
2. `handoff.md` deterministically reflects the record's fields.
3. Legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json` records still load.
4. Focused test asserts folder creation + markdown content + JSON round-trip.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten subticket.
- Related: narrative payload `6431985e` (richer md), delta form `96f9ffaa`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
arguments.title: [session-api][handoff] Store handoffs as folders with handoff.json + rendered handoff.md

arguments.description:
# Goal

Make durable session artifacts persist in git for later feedback/research loops, while keeping machine-local pointers/locks ignored (subticket 4 of the flattening tracker). Depends on the flattened layout + handoff folders.

# Problem (verified)

- Root `.gitignore`:
  ```
  .session/*
  !.session/sessions/
  !.session/sessions/default/**
  .session/sessions/**/session-capture-stop.log
  ```
  Tracks `sessions/**` (222 files tracked) but never re-includes `runtime/`, so handoffs/context/finish are git-ignored.
- `memory-api/.gitignore:5` ignores `.session/` wholesale (inconsistent with root).

# Solution Design

1. Rewrite root `.gitignore` to track durable artifacts under `sessions/<session_id>/` (`context.json`, `handoffs/**`, `finish.json`, `runs/**/transcript.json`) and ignore only `.session/local/` and `*.lock` + capture logs.
2. Align `memory-api/.gitignore` with the same policy (stop ignoring the whole `.session/`).
3. Split tracking by **durability, not directory**: local pointer + lock ignored; everything session-owned tracked.
4. Guard repo bloat: land event-capture de-dup (`67d7c279`) before bulk-tracking `events.json`; decide whether `runs/**/events.json` is tracked or ignored (recommend track transcript, keep events optional/ignored initially).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New handoffs/context/finish under `sessions/<session_id>/` are git-tracked.
2. `.session/local/` pointers and `*.lock` remain ignored.
3. Root and `memory-api` `.gitignore` express one consistent policy.
4. A documented decision records the events.json tracking choice and its size rationale.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten + handoff-folder subtickets.
- Prerequisite: event de-dup `67d7c279` (land before bulk-tracking events).
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
arguments.title: [session-api][store] Git-tracking policy: track durable session artifacts, ignore local pointers/locks

arguments.description:
# Goal

Make durable session artifacts persist in git for later feedback/research loops, while keeping machine-local pointers/locks ignored (subticket 4 of the flattening tracker). Depends on the flattened layout + handoff folders.

# Problem (verified)

- Root `.gitignore`:
  ```
  .session/*
  !.session/sessions/
  !.session/sessions/default/**
  .session/sessions/**/session-capture-stop.log
  ```
  Tracks `sessions/**` (222 files tracked) but never re-includes `runtime/`, so handoffs/context/finish are git-ignored.
- `memory-api/.gitignore:5` ignores `.session/` wholesale (inconsistent with root).

# Solution Design

1. Rewrite root `.gitignore` to track durable artifacts under `sessions/<session_id>/` (`context.json`, `handoffs/**`, `finish.json`, `runs/**/transcript.json`) and ignore only `.session/local/` and `*.lock` + capture logs.
2. Align `memory-api/.gitignore` with the same policy (stop ignoring the whole `.session/`).
3. Split tracking by **durability, not directory**: local pointer + lock ignored; everything session-owned tracked.
4. Guard repo bloat: land event-capture de-dup (`67d7c279`) before bulk-tracking `events.json`; decide whether `runs/**/events.json` is tracked or ignored (recommend track transcript, keep events optional/ignored initially).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New handoffs/context/finish under `sessions/<session_id>/` are git-tracked.
2. `.session/local/` pointers and `*.lock` remain ignored.
3. Root and `memory-api` `.gitignore` express one consistent policy.
4. A documented decision records the events.json tracking choice and its size rationale.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten + handoff-folder subtickets.
- Prerequisite: event de-dup `67d7c279` (land before bulk-tracking events).
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

arguments.description:
# Goal

Collapse the `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` layer so a session **owns** its runtime context and handoffs directly, all sessions share the single `.session/` store, and durable artifacts are git-tracked for feedback/research loops. **Tracker parent — decomposed into 4 subtickets. This track gates all further session-api handoff/schema work.**

# Problem (evidence)

Verified in `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`:

- Two decoupled ID spaces: captured sessions live at `.session/sessions/<session_id>/` (`paths_for_session_id`, store.rs:1717); runtime threads live at `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` (`runtime_paths_for_workspace`, store.rs:1754) holding `context.json`, `handoffs/`, `finish.json`.
- `SessionRuntimeContext` (`model.rs:51`) and `SessionRunLineage` carry **no `session_id`**; `SessionLinks` (`model.rs:298`) links to tickets/specs/docs/logs but **not** to a runtime thread. There is no join field between the two trees.
- Relationship is 1 thread : N captured transcripts (resume reuses `workspace_session_id`, appends a `run_id`; each capture is a fresh `session_id`).
- Root `.gitignore` (`.session/*` + only un-ignoring `sessions/`) means `runtime/` — **handoffs, context, finish — is git-ignored**, exactly the artifacts wanted for research loops. `memory-api/.gitignore:5` ignores `.session/` entirely.

# Design (target layout)

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/        (git-tracked)
    context.json                       pins + workflow + run lineage
    handoffs/<handoff_id>/handoff.json + handoff.md
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/{transcript.json, events.json}
.session/local/                        (git-ignored)
    active_session.json                current-thread pointer
    *.lock
```

# Decomposition

1. Unify identity (add `session_id` join) — foundational.
2. Flatten layout + relocate local-only pointers/locks, with back-compat readers.
3. Handoffs as folders (`handoff.json` + rendered `handoff.md`).
4. Git-tracking policy (root + `memory-api` `.gitignore`).

# What must be preserved (verified reasons NOT to over-collapse)

- One directory **per continuity thread** (pins/workflow/lineage/finish are cumulative per thread; multiple threads already coexist).
- Finish immutability per thread (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`, store.rs:717).
- Local-only pointers (`active_workspace_session.json`) and the mutation lock stay git-ignored.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A session owns its `context.json`, `handoffs/`, and `finish.json` directly under `sessions/<session_id>/`; `runtime/workspaces/` is no longer written.
2. Runtime context and captured transcripts share the `session_id` join.
3. Handoffs persist as folders with JSON + markdown.
4. Durable artifacts are git-tracked; local pointers/locks are ignored.
5. Existing `runtime/workspaces/` records still load (back-compat).

# Traceability

- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.
- Gates further work: `6431985e`, `96f9ffaa`, `0d3fdba6`, `e731d333`.

arguments.description:
# Goal

Establish the missing join between a runtime continuity thread and its captured transcripts so handoffs and context can be session-owned (subticket 1 of the flattening tracker). Foundational — no directory move yet.

# Problem (verified)

- `SessionRuntimeContext` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:51`) has `workspace_session_id` but no `session_id`.
- `SessionRunLineage` (`model.rs:43`) has `run_id` + `predecessor_run_id` but no captured-`session_id`.
- Captured sessions (`SessionLinks`, `model.rs:298`) do not reference a thread/run.
- So "handoff owned by the session" is currently undefined — there is nothing tying a transcript to a runtime thread/run.

# Solution Design

1. Introduce a canonical `session_id` for the continuity thread. Simplest: make `workspace_session_id` the session id (alias) or add `session_id` to `SessionRuntimeContext` populated at `init_runtime_context` (store.rs:608).
2. Stamp each captured transcript with its owning `session_id` + `run_id`: extend `SessionRunLineage` (or the capture manifest) so a run records the captured `session_id`, and the capture path (`capture_copilot_hook`) records the active `run_id`/thread.
3. Provide a resolver `runs_for_session(session_id) -> Vec<run>` and `session_for_run(run_id)` so readers can navigate the join.
4. Keep serde defaults so existing records without the join still deserialize.

# Non-goals

- Moving files on disk (subticket 2).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Runtime context exposes a stable `session_id` for the thread.
2. Each run records its captured `session_id`; the join is navigable both directions.
3. Old records without the join deserialize with empty/derived defaults.
4. Focused test asserts the thread↔run↔transcript join round-trips.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
arguments.title: [session-api][store] Unify session identity: link runtime context to session_id and stamp transcripts

arguments.description:
# Goal

Relocate runtime context/handoffs/finish to be owned directly by `sessions/<session_id>/`, move local-only pointers/locks to `.session/local/`, and remove the `runtime/workspaces/` nesting (subticket 2 of the flattening tracker). Depends on identity unification.

# Problem (verified)

- `runtime_paths_for_workspace` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1754`) builds `runtime/workspaces/<id>/{context.json,handoffs,finish.json}`.
- `runtime_root` (store.rs:1743) and `active_workspace_session_path` (store.rs:1751) place a machine-local "current thread" pointer in the same ignored tree.
- Captured transcripts live at `sessions/<session_id>/` (`paths_for_session_id`, store.rs:1717).

# Target layout

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/
    context.json
    handoffs/…            (folder form in subticket 3)
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/{transcript.json, events.json}
.session/local/
    active_session.json
    *.lock
```

# Solution Design

1. Rework the path builders so `context.json`/`handoffs/`/`finish.json` resolve under `sessions/<session_id>/`; move captured `transcript.json`/`events.json` under `runs/<run_id>/`.
2. Move the active-thread pointer and mutation lock to `.session/local/` (never git-tracked).
3. Add a back-compat read fallback: if `sessions/<id>/context.json` is absent, read the legacy `runtime/workspaces/<id>/` location. Provide an opt-in migration that relocates legacy threads.
4. Update all readers/writers (`persist_runtime_context`, finish, handoff, capture) and CLI/MCP path outputs.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New writes land under `sessions/<session_id>/`; nothing writes to `runtime/workspaces/`.
2. Local pointer + lock live under `.session/local/`.
3. Legacy `runtime/workspaces/` records still load via fallback; migration relocates them.
4. Finish immutability semantics unchanged (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`).
5. Focused tests cover new-layout round-trip + legacy fallback.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on identity subticket.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

arguments.description:
# Goal

Store each handoff as a folder containing the canonical `handoff.json` plus a rendered `handoff.md`, so markdown handoffs sit next to the JSON for human review, feedback, and research loops (subticket 3 of the flattening tracker). Depends on the layout flatten.

# Problem (verified)

- `create_handoff_record` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1343`) writes a single flat file `handoffs/<handoff_id>.json`.
- A terminal render already exists (`render_handoff_record_terminal`, store.rs:2108) but is never persisted — the human-readable form is thrown away.

# Solution Design

1. Change the handoff writer to create a folder `handoffs/<handoff_id>/` and write:
   - `handoff.json` — canonical record (unchanged schema, or delta form per `96f9ffaa`).
   - `handoff.md` — deterministic markdown rendering of the record.
2. Add a `render_handoff_record_markdown(&SessionHandoffRecord) -> String` producing headed sections (summary, remaining, decisions, blockers, pins, workflow, validation, resume command) — reuse the terminal renderer's structure.
3. Update `create_handoff_result.record_path` and CLI/MCP outputs to point at the folder (and/or the `.md`).
4. Back-compat read: still resolve legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json`.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A new handoff persists a folder with both `handoff.json` and `handoff.md`.
2. `handoff.md` deterministically reflects the record's fields.
3. Legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json` records still load.
4. Focused test asserts folder creation + markdown content + JSON round-trip.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten subticket.
- Related: narrative payload `6431985e` (richer md), delta form `96f9ffaa`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

arguments.description:
# Goal

Collapse the `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` layer so a session **owns** its runtime context and handoffs directly, all sessions share the single `.session/` store, and durable artifacts are git-tracked for feedback/research loops. **Tracker parent — decomposed into 4 subtickets. This track gates all further session-api handoff/schema work.**

# Problem (evidence)

Verified in `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`:

- Two decoupled ID spaces: captured sessions live at `.session/sessions/<session_id>/` (`paths_for_session_id`, store.rs:1717); runtime threads live at `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` (`runtime_paths_for_workspace`, store.rs:1754) holding `context.json`, `handoffs/`, `finish.json`.
- `SessionRuntimeContext` (`model.rs:51`) and `SessionRunLineage` carry **no `session_id`**; `SessionLinks` (`model.rs:298`) links to tickets/specs/docs/logs but **not** to a runtime thread. There is no join field between the two trees.
- Relationship is 1 thread : N captured transcripts (resume reuses `workspace_session_id`, appends a `run_id`; each capture is a fresh `session_id`).
- Root `.gitignore` (`.session/*` + only un-ignoring `sessions/`) means `runtime/` — **handoffs, context, finish — is git-ignored**, exactly the artifacts wanted for research loops. `memory-api/.gitignore:5` ignores `.session/` entirely.

# Design (target layout)

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/        (git-tracked)
    context.json                       pins + workflow + run lineage
    handoffs/<handoff_id>/handoff.json + handoff.md
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/{transcript.json, events.json}
.session/local/                        (git-ignored)
    active_session.json                current-thread pointer
    *.lock
```

# Decomposition

1. Unify identity (add `session_id` join) — foundational.
2. Flatten layout + relocate local-only pointers/locks, with back-compat readers.
3. Handoffs as folders (`handoff.json` + rendered `handoff.md`).
4. Git-tracking policy (root + `memory-api` `.gitignore`).

# What must be preserved (verified reasons NOT to over-collapse)

- One directory **per continuity thread** (pins/workflow/lineage/finish are cumulative per thread; multiple threads already coexist).
- Finish immutability per thread (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`, store.rs:717).
- Local-only pointers (`active_workspace_session.json`) and the mutation lock stay git-ignored.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A session owns its `context.json`, `handoffs/`, and `finish.json` directly under `sessions/<session_id>/`; `runtime/workspaces/` is no longer written.
2. Runtime context and captured transcripts share the `session_id` join.
3. Handoffs persist as folders with JSON + markdown.
4. Durable artifacts are git-tracked; local pointers/locks are ignored.
5. Existing `runtime/workspaces/` records still load (back-compat).

# Traceability

- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.
- Gates further work: `6431985e`, `96f9ffaa`, `0d3fdba6`, `e731d333`.
arguments.title: [session-api][store] Flatten session store layout, unify identity, and git-track durable artifacts

tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Store each handoff as a folder containing the canonical `handoff.json` plus a rendered `handoff.md`, so markdown handoffs sit next to the JSON for human review, feedback, and research loops (subticket 3 of the flattening tracker). Depends on the layout flatten.

# Problem (verified)

- `create_handoff_record` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1343`) writes a single flat file `handoffs/<handoff_id>.json`.
- A terminal render already exists (`render_handoff_record_terminal`, store.rs:2108) but is never persisted — the human-readable form is thrown away.

# Solution Design

1. Change the handoff writer to create a folder `handoffs/<handoff_id>/` and write:
   - `handoff.json` — canonical record (unchanged schema, or delta form per `96f9ffaa`).
   - `handoff.md` — deterministic markdown rendering of the record.
2. Add a `render_handoff_record_markdown(&SessionHandoffRecord) -> String` producing headed sections (summary, remaining, decisions, blockers, pins, workflow, validation, resume command) — reuse the terminal renderer's structure.
3. Update `create_handoff_result.record_path` and CLI/MCP outputs to point at the folder (and/or the `.md`).
4. Back-compat read: still resolve legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json`.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A new handoff persists a folder with both `handoff.json` and `handoff.md`.
2. `handoff.md` deterministically reflects the record's fields.
3. Legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json` records still load.
4. Focused test asserts folder creation + markdown content + JSON round-trip.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten subticket.
- Related: narrative payload `6431985e` (richer md), delta form `96f9ffaa`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Relocate runtime context/handoffs/finish to be owned directly by `sessions/<session_id>/`, move local-only pointers/locks to `.session/local/`, and remove the `runtime/workspaces/` nesting (subticket 2 of the flattening tracker). Depends on identity unification.

# Problem (verified)

- `runtime_paths_for_workspace` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1754`) builds `runtime/workspaces/<id>/{context.json,handoffs,finish.json}`.
- `runtime_root` (store.rs:1743) and `active_workspace_session_path` (store.rs:1751) place a machine-local "current thread" pointer in the same ignored tree.
- Captured transcripts live at `sessions/<session_id>/` (`paths_for_session_id`, store.rs:1717).

# Target layout

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/
    context.json
    handoffs/…            (folder form in subticket 3)
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/{transcript.json, events.json}
.session/local/
    active_session.json
    *.lock
```

# Solution Design

1. Rework the path builders so `context.json`/`handoffs/`/`finish.json` resolve under `sessions/<session_id>/`; move captured `transcript.json`/`events.json` under `runs/<run_id>/`.
2. Move the active-thread pointer and mutation lock to `.session/local/` (never git-tracked).
3. Add a back-compat read fallback: if `sessions/<id>/context.json` is absent, read the legacy `runtime/workspaces/<id>/` location. Provide an opt-in migration that relocates legacy threads.
4. Update all readers/writers (`persist_runtime_context`, finish, handoff, capture) and CLI/MCP path outputs.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New writes land under `sessions/<session_id>/`; nothing writes to `runtime/workspaces/`.
2. Local pointer + lock live under `.session/local/`.
3. Legacy `runtime/workspaces/` records still load via fallback; migration relocates them.
4. Finish immutability semantics unchanged (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`).
5. Focused tests cover new-layout round-trip + legacy fallback.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on identity subticket.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Establish the missing join between a runtime continuity thread and its captured transcripts so handoffs and context can be session-owned (subticket 1 of the flattening tracker). Foundational — no directory move yet.

# Problem (verified)

- `SessionRuntimeContext` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs:51`) has `workspace_session_id` but no `session_id`.
- `SessionRunLineage` (`model.rs:43`) has `run_id` + `predecessor_run_id` but no captured-`session_id`.
- Captured sessions (`SessionLinks`, `model.rs:298`) do not reference a thread/run.
- So "handoff owned by the session" is currently undefined — there is nothing tying a transcript to a runtime thread/run.

# Solution Design

1. Introduce a canonical `session_id` for the continuity thread. Simplest: make `workspace_session_id` the session id (alias) or add `session_id` to `SessionRuntimeContext` populated at `init_runtime_context` (store.rs:608).
2. Stamp each captured transcript with its owning `session_id` + `run_id`: extend `SessionRunLineage` (or the capture manifest) so a run records the captured `session_id`, and the capture path (`capture_copilot_hook`) records the active `run_id`/thread.
3. Provide a resolver `runs_for_session(session_id) -> Vec<run>` and `session_for_run(run_id)` so readers can navigate the join.
4. Keep serde defaults so existing records without the join still deserialize.

# Non-goals

- Moving files on disk (subticket 2).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. Runtime context exposes a stable `session_id` for the thread.
2. Each run records its captured `session_id`; the join is navigable both directions.
3. Old records without the join deserialize with empty/derived defaults.
4. Focused test asserts the thread↔run↔transcript join round-trips.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
tool_arguments_json.title: [session-api][store] Unify session identity: link runtime context to session_id and stamp transcripts

tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Make durable session artifacts persist in git for later feedback/research loops, while keeping machine-local pointers/locks ignored (subticket 4 of the flattening tracker). Depends on the flattened layout + handoff folders.

# Problem (verified)

- Root `.gitignore`:
  ```
  .session/*
  !.session/sessions/
  !.session/sessions/default/**
  .session/sessions/**/session-capture-stop.log
  ```
  Tracks `sessions/**` (222 files tracked) but never re-includes `runtime/`, so handoffs/context/finish are git-ignored.
- `memory-api/.gitignore:5` ignores `.session/` wholesale (inconsistent with root).

# Solution Design

1. Rewrite root `.gitignore` to track durable artifacts under `sessions/<session_id>/` (`context.json`, `handoffs/**`, `finish.json`, `runs/**/transcript.json`) and ignore only `.session/local/` and `*.lock` + capture logs.
2. Align `memory-api/.gitignore` with the same policy (stop ignoring the whole `.session/`).
3. Split tracking by **durability, not directory**: local pointer + lock ignored; everything session-owned tracked.
4. Guard repo bloat: land event-capture de-dup (`67d7c279`) before bulk-tracking `events.json`; decide whether `runs/**/events.json` is tracked or ignored (recommend track transcript, keep events optional/ignored initially).

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New handoffs/context/finish under `sessions/<session_id>/` are git-tracked.
2. `.session/local/` pointers and `*.lock` remain ignored.
3. Root and `memory-api` `.gitignore` express one consistent policy.
4. A documented decision records the events.json tracking choice and its size rationale.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten + handoff-folder subtickets.
- Prerequisite: event de-dup `67d7c279` (land before bulk-tracking events).
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. New handoffs/context/finish under `sessions/<session_id>/` are git-tracked.
2. `.session/local/` pointers and `*.lock` remain ignored.
3. Root and `memory-api` `.gitignore` express one consistent policy.
4. A documented decision records the events.json tracking choice and its size rationale.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten + handoff-folder subtickets.
- Prerequisite: event de-dup `67d7c279` (land before bulk-tracking events).
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
arguments.title: [session-api][store] Git-tracking policy: track durable session artifacts, ignore local pointers/locks

tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Collapse the `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` layer so a session **owns** its runtime context and handoffs directly, all sessions share the single `.session/` store, and durable artifacts are git-tracked for feedback/research loops. **Tracker parent — decomposed into 4 subtickets. This track gates all further session-api handoff/schema work.**

# Problem (evidence)

Verified in `memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs`:

- Two decoupled ID spaces: captured sessions live at `.session/sessions/<session_id>/` (`paths_for_session_id`, store.rs:1717); runtime threads live at `.session/runtime/workspaces/<workspace_session_id>/` (`runtime_paths_for_workspace`, store.rs:1754) holding `context.json`, `handoffs/`, `finish.json`.
- `SessionRuntimeContext` (`model.rs:51`) and `SessionRunLineage` carry **no `session_id`**; `SessionLinks` (`model.rs:298`) links to tickets/specs/docs/logs but **not** to a runtime thread. There is no join field between the two trees.
- Relationship is 1 thread : N captured transcripts (resume reuses `workspace_session_id`, appends a `run_id`; each capture is a fresh `session_id`).
- Root `.gitignore` (`.session/*` + only un-ignoring `sessions/`) means `runtime/` — **handoffs, context, finish — is git-ignored**, exactly the artifacts wanted for research loops. `memory-api/.gitignore:5` ignores `.session/` entirely.

# Design (target layout)

```
.session/sessions/<session_id>/        (git-tracked)
    context.json                       pins + workflow + run lineage
    handoffs/<handoff_id>/handoff.json + handoff.md
    finish.json
    runs/<run_id>/{transcript.json, events.json}
.session/local/                        (git-ignored)
    active_session.json                current-thread pointer
    *.lock
```

# Decomposition

1. Unify identity (add `session_id` join) — foundational.
2. Flatten layout + relocate local-only pointers/locks, with back-compat readers.
3. Handoffs as folders (`handoff.json` + rendered `handoff.md`).
4. Git-tracking policy (root + `memory-api` `.gitignore`).

# What must be preserved (verified reasons NOT to over-collapse)

- One directory **per continuity thread** (pins/workflow/lineage/finish are cumulative per thread; multiple threads already coexist).
- Finish immutability per thread (`ensure_workspace_not_finished`, store.rs:717).
- Local-only pointers (`active_workspace_session.json`) and the mutation lock stay git-ignored.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A session owns its `context.json`, `handoffs/`, and `finish.json` directly under `sessions/<session_id>/`; `runtime/workspaces/` is no longer written.
2. Runtime context and captured transcripts share the `session_id` join.
3. Handoffs persist as folders with JSON + markdown.
4. Durable artifacts are git-tracked; local pointers/locks are ignored.
5. Existing `runtime/workspaces/` records still load (back-compat).

# Traceability

- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
- Epic: `effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8`.
- Gates further work: `6431985e`, `96f9ffaa`, `0d3fdba6`, `e731d333`.

arguments.title: [session-api][store] Flatten session store layout, unify identity, and git-track durable artifacts

tool_arguments_json.description:
# Goal

Store each handoff as a folder containing the canonical `handoff.json` plus a rendered `handoff.md`, so markdown handoffs sit next to the JSON for human review, feedback, and research loops (subticket 3 of the flattening tracker). Depends on the layout flatten.

# Problem (verified)

- `create_handoff_record` (`memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs:1343`) writes a single flat file `handoffs/<handoff_id>.json`.
- A terminal render already exists (`render_handoff_record_terminal`, store.rs:2108) but is never persisted — the human-readable form is thrown away.

# Solution Design

1. Change the handoff writer to create a folder `handoffs/<handoff_id>/` and write:
   - `handoff.json` — canonical record (unchanged schema, or delta form per `96f9ffaa`).
   - `handoff.md` — deterministic markdown rendering of the record.
2. Add a `render_handoff_record_markdown(&SessionHandoffRecord) -> String` producing headed sections (summary, remaining, decisions, blockers, pins, workflow, validation, resume command) — reuse the terminal renderer's structure.
3. Update `create_handoff_result.record_path` and CLI/MCP outputs to point at the folder (and/or the `.md`).
4. Back-compat read: still resolve legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json`.

# Acceptance Criteria

1. A new handoff persists a folder with both `handoff.json` and `handoff.md`.
2. `handoff.md` deterministically reflects the record's fields.
3. Legacy flat `handoffs/<id>.json` records still load.
4. Focused test asserts folder creation + markdown content + JSON round-trip.

# Traceability

- Parent: flattening tracker. Depends on layout-flatten subticket.
- Related: narrative payload `6431985e` (richer md), delta form `96f9ffaa`.
- Spec: `8c880efc-7083-4e1d-bf06-96b8254be913`.
